gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/399)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/399)
text: دَخَلْتُ عَلَى جَمَاعَةٍ مِنْ بَنِي هَاشِمٍ فَسَلَّمْتُ عَلَيْهِمْ فِي بَيْتٍ مُظْلِمٍ فَقَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ سَلِّمْ عَلَى أَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) فَإِنَّهُ فِي الصَّدْرِ قَالَ فَسَلَّمْتُ عَلَيْهِ وَ جَلَسْتُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ قَدْ أَحْبَبْتُ أَنْ أَلْقَاكَ مُنْذُ حِينٍ لِأَسْأَلَكَ عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ فَقَالَ سَلْ مَا بَدَا لَكَ قُلْتُ مَا تَقُولُ فِي الْحَمَّامِ قَالَ لَا تَدْخُلِ الْحَمَّامَ إِلَّا بِمِئْزَرٍ وَ غُضَّ بَصَرَكَ وَ لَا تَغْتَسِلْ مِنْ غُسَالَةِ مَاءِ الْحَمَّامِ فَإِنَّهُ يُغْتَسَلُ فِيهِ مِنَ الزِّنَا وَ يَغْتَسِلُ فِيهِ وَلَدُ الزِّنَا وَ النَّاصِبُ لَنَا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ وَ هُوَ شَرُّهُمْ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam,

(It has been narrated) from a man from the Clan of Hashimas who said, ‘I went over to a group from the Clan of Hashimas, so I greeted them in a dark room. So one of them said, ‘Greet Abu Al Hassanasws, for heasws is in the centre’. So I greeted himasws and sat in front of himasws, and I said to himasws, ‘I desired to meet youasws for a while to ask youasws about certain things’. So heasws said: ‘Ask, whatever comes to you’. I said, ‘What are youasws saying regarding the bathhouse’. Heasws said: ‘Do not enter the bathhouse except with a loincloth, and lowering your eyes, and do not wash from the used water of the bathhouse for there has washed in it the adulterer, and the son of the adulterer, and the Hostile one (Nasibi) towards usasws, the Peopleasws of the Household, and he is the most evil of them’.
en.sarwar: 9. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Ali ibn abu Hamzah who has said the following: “I once entered a bathhouse with abu Basir and saw abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, who had applied (lime) and on his underarms. I then informed abu Basir and he asked me to lead him so he can ask him about it. I said, ‘I saw him.’ He said, ‘You saw him (the Imam) but I cannot see him (the Imam)? Lead me to where he is.’ I then led him and he said, ‘I pray to Allah to keep my soul in service for your cause, my lead has said that you have applied lime and on your underarms.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, O abu Muhammad, pulling up hairs of underarms weakens eyesight, so you must apply lime, O abu Muhammad.’ He (the narrator) has said, ‘I applied since that time and he (the Imam) said, ‘Apply; it is cleansing.’”
